bazel.executable.description: The name of the Bazel executable. This may be an absolute path, or a simple name that will be searched for on the system path. If empty, "bazel" on the system path will be used.

bazel.buildifierExecutable.markdownDescription:
The name of the Buildifier executable. This may be an absolute path, or a simple name that will be searched for on the system path. Paths starting with `@` are interpreted as Bazel targets and are run via `bazel run`. If empty, "buildifier" on the system path will be used.

Buildifier can be downloaded from https://github.com/bazelbuild/buildtools/releases.

bazel.buildifierConfigJsonPath.markdownDescription: The path of the Buildifier config json file, ex: `.buildifier.json`. This may be an absolute path, or a relative path within the workspace. If this option is unset, `buildifier` will automatically look for a `.buildifier.json` file in the workspace.

bazel.buildifierFixOnFormat.description: Whether to automatically apply lint fixes from buildifier when formatting a Bazel file.

bazel.queriesShareServer.description: Use the same Bazel server for queries and builds. By default, vscode-bazel uses the same server for queries and builds to make sure this extension works out-of-the-box. However, you may experience degraded performance in Visual Studio Code for operations that require queries. You can disable this setting so that queries and builds can be executed in parallel in some circumstances. See https://github.com/bazelbuild/vscode-bazel?tab=readme-ov-file#using-a-separate-output-base for more information.

bazel.queryOutputBase.description: If queriesShareServer is false, this sets the directory in which bazel will create its output_base directory. Defaults to $TMPDIR.

bazel.enableCodeLens.markdownDescription: Whether to add a CodeLens to `BUILD`/`BUILD.bazel` files to provide actions while browsing the file.

bazel.pathsToIgnore.description: A list of Regexs that when matched cause paths not to be checked for being in a WORKSPACE. This impacts operations that require a workspace, like queries, but syntax highlighting and buildifier work regardless.

bazel.commandLine.startupOptions.markdownDescription: A list of [Bazel startup options](https://docs.bazel.build/versions/master/command-line-reference.html#startup-options) to use when building, running tests, running queries, or cleaning. One option per entry, no shell escaping is needed.

bazel.commandLine.commandArgs.markdownDescription: A list of Bazel command line options to use when building or running tests. From the [common options](https://docs.bazel.build/versions/master/command-line-reference.html#options-common-to-all-commands) and [build options](https://docs.bazel.build/versions/master/command-line-reference.html#build) (`test` honors all `build` options). One option per entry, no shell escaping is needed.

bazel.commandLine.queryExpression.description: A [query language expression](https://bazel.build/query/language) which determines the packages and targets displayed in the workspace tree and quick picker. The default inspects the entire workspace, but you could narrow it. For example: `//part/you/want/...:*` or `kind('.*_test rule', //...)`

bazel.lsp.command.description: The executable to launch for the LSP (experimental). Providing this disables certain features in the extension on the basis that they'll be provided by this language server.

bazel.lsp.args.description: The arguments to pass to the LSP executable

bazel.lsp.env.description: Environment variables to pass to the LSP executable

bazel.trace.server.description: Trace the communication between VS Code and Bazel LSP server (language server).

args.description: Target labels and other command line options passed to the 'bazel build' command.

bazelCommand.description: The Bazel command to execute (e.g., build, test, etc.). Defaults to "build".

bazelExecutablePath.description: The name of the Bazel executable. This may be an absolute path, or a simple name that will be searched for on the system path. If empty, "bazel" on the system path will be used.

cwd.description: The working directory in which Bazel will be launched.

port.description: The port number of the Bazel debug server.

verbose.description: Show verbose logs for the debugger.

command.description: The Bazel command to execute ("build" or "test").

targets.description: The labels of the targets to build or test.

options.description: Additional command line parameters to pass to Bazel.
